segments.lat: male me hercule de Athamante. tuus autem dolor humanus is quidem sed magno opere moderandus. consolationum autem multae viae sed illa rectissima: impetret ratio quod dies impetratura est. Alexin vero curemus, imaginem Tironis, quem aegrum Romam remisi, et, si quid habet collis ἐπιδήμιον , ad me cum †testamento† transferamus. tota domus vacat superior, ut scis. hoc puto valde ad rem pertinere.
segments.eng: Bad news, by Hercules, about Athamas. Your own grief is human, certainly, but to be kept within bounds. There are many roads of consolation, but the straightest is this: that reason should win the point that time would win in the end. As for Alexis, let us take care of him --- the very image of Tiro --- whom I have sent back to Rome ill; and if the hill carries any [Greek: epid\=emion] --- any local infection --- let us shift him to me along with \dag Tisamenus\dag. The whole upper part of the house is empty, as you know. I think this matters greatly to the case.
